Copaiba officinalis, also known as Copaiba tree, is a large evergreen tree native to the Amazon rainforest and other parts of South America. The plant is valued for its resin, which is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues, wounds, and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Copaiba officinalis include copalic acid, beta-caryophyllene, and other sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Copaiba officinalis is commonly prepared as an essential oil, resin extract, or tincture, which can be used to make herbal preparations such as salves, ointments, and topical creams.
